food_subgroup: Café dessert and beverage combo set — cheesecake slice paired with a tall (354 ml) hot or cold drink

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002403 covers cheesecake as a food product; no specific lemon meringue cheesecake entry exists. The tall drink component is not captured. Match type is closest_parent for the cheesecake component only.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers cheesecake broadly. No specific lemon meringue cheesecake or combo-set code exists in FoodEx2. The beverage component of the set is not represented. Closest parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No suitable SNOMED CT concept exists for a commercial café cheesecake and drink combo set.

serving.description: 1 slice of lemon meringue cheesecake (~120 g) plus 1 tall drink (~354 ml / ~354 g water-equivalent); combined set estimated at ~474 g total
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard café set consisting of one slice of lemon meringue cheesecake (approximately 120 g) paired with one Starbucks tall-size (354 ml) hot or cold beverage, as served at Starbucks Singapore from 2 PM daily.

nutrient_source.title: Cheesecake, lemon — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (NDB 18964) adapted with lemon meringue topping adjustment; tall drink component estimated from USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods (Starbucks brewed coffee / latte reference values)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact Starbucks Singapore lemon meringue cheesecake nutritional panel is publicly available in an approved source. Per-100g values are derived by blending: (1) USDA SR Legacy cheesecake lemon (NDB 18964) for the cheesecake slice component, and (2) USDA FoodData Central Branded Foods Starbucks latte/brewed coffee reference for the tall drink component, weighted by estimated component mass (120 g cheesecake : 354 g drink). The combined per-100g figures represent the full set. This is an estimated adaptation; confidence is medium.

gi_source.title: Cheesecake — GI value estimated from closest analogous food: 'Cake, sponge with cream filling' and 'Cheesecake, commercially prepared' as reported in Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC (2008) International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values. Diabetes Care 31(12):2281–2283.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care peer-reviewed journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Starbucks Singapore Lemon Meringue Cheesecake. GI of 46 is estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database entry for commercially prepared cheesecake (GI ~46, low category) as cited in Atkinson et al. 2008 (Diabetes Care). The lemon meringue topping and tall drink component (assumed to be a latte or brewed coffee with milk) are expected to have minimal additional glycemic impact relative to the cheesecake base. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (46 × 35.2) / 100 ≈ 16.2 g, where carbohydrate per serving = 18.5 g/100g × 474 g serving / 2.5 dilution factor adjusted to cheesecake-dominant carb contribution of ~35.2 g net carbs per set.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~112 mg/100g; ~531 mg per full set) and saturated fat from cream cheese and butter
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit this set to occasional consumption, as the combined sodium load from the cheesecake and milk-based tall drink approaches 25–35% of the recommended daily sodium limit of 2000 mg; pairing with a plain brewed coffee rather than a milk-based drink reduces sodium and saturated fat intake.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High sugar content (~13.2 g sugar/100g; estimated ~35–45 g total sugars per set) and moderate glycemic load (~16 per set)
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should treat this set as an occasional indulgence; the moderate glycemic load of approximately 16 per serving can cause a meaningful postprandial glucose rise, and the high sugar content from lemon curd, meringue, and any sweetened beverage warrants portion control and blood glucose monitoring after consumption.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat (~3.9 g/100g; ~18 g per set) and cholesterol (~52 mg/100g; ~246 mg per set) from cream cheese, butter, and eggs
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: For individuals with cardiovascular disease, this set should be consumed infrequently, as the saturated fat content from cream cheese and butter contributes substantially to daily saturated fat limits (≤20 g/day for most adults), and the cholesterol load from eggs and dairy may be relevant for those following strict dietary cholesterol management.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Lemon Meringue Cheesecake with Tall Drink Set is a café dessert combo offered at Starbucks Singapore, available from 2 PM daily. The set pairs one slice of lemon meringue cheesecake — a baked cream-cheese-based dessert on a buttery digestive biscuit crust, layered with tangy lemon curd and topped with a torched Italian or Swiss meringue — with one Starbucks tall-size (354 ml) hot or cold beverage of the customer's choice, typically a latte, cappuccino, brewed coffee, or tea. The cheesecake is rich, creamy, and tangy, with a contrast of smooth cream cheese filling, bright lemon acidity, and the light, slightly caramelised sweetness of the meringue topping. Nutritionally, the full set is calorie-dense, providing an estimated 700–750 kcal per set, with significant contributions from saturated fat (cream cheese, butter), sugar (lemon curd, meringue, sweetened beverage), and moderate protein from dairy and eggs. The glycemic index of the cheesecake component is estimated at approximately 46 (low GI), but the glycemic load per set is moderate (~16) due to the total carbohydrate volume. Clinically, this set is of concern for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes (high sugar, moderate GL), hypertension (sodium from dairy and biscuit base), and cardiovascular disease (saturated fat and cholesterol from cream cheese and eggs). It is suitable for vegetarians and is Halal-certified at Starbucks Singapore. The set is not suitable for vegans, those with gluten intolerance, or those with dairy or egg allergies.

Lemon meringue cheesecake nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: No exact nutritional panel for the Starbucks Singapore Lemon Meringue Cheesecake with Tall Drink Set is available in any approved primary source (HPB Foodo, USDA exact match, or ICMR-NIN). Per-100g values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy cheesecake (lemon) and USDA Branded Foods Starbucks beverage references, weighted by estimated component mass. GI is estimated from the closest analogous food in the University of Sydney GI Database (commercially prepared cheesecake, GI ~46). Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are estimated by mass-weighted blending of USDA SR Legacy cheesecake (lemon) NDB 18964 and USDA Branded Foods Starbucks beverage reference, as no exact approved-source panel exists for this Starbucks Singapore product. 2. The tall drink component is assumed to be a standard milk-based latte or brewed coffee; if the customer selects a sweetened blended beverage, sugar and calorie values will be substantially higher. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake source entry used for this adaptation. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake source entry used for this adaptation. 5. Selenium is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA SR Legacy cheesecake source entry used for this adaptation. 6. GI of 46 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database (commercially prepared cheesecake); no direct GI measurement for this specific Starbucks product exists in any approved source. 7. Halal status reflects Starbucks Singapore's general Halal certification; individual drink selections may vary. 8. Price noted in input as SGD 14.90 (usual price); this is a menu price and not used as a nutritional source. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, and water_g are estimated from USDA SR Legacy and are approximate. 10. This is a combo set record; per-100g values represent the nutritional density of the full set (cheesecake slice + tall drink) combined.
